title: Epidemiologists
description: Investigate and describe the determinants and distribution of disease, disability, or health outcomes. May develop the means for prevention and control.

tasks.text: Communicate research findings on various types of diseases to health practitioners, policy makers, and the public.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Oversee public health programs, including statistical analysis, health care planning, surveillance systems, and public health improvement.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause and risk factors, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate healthcare workers, patients, and the public about infectious and communicable diseases, including disease transmission and prevention.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor and report incidents of infectious diseases to local and state health agencies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Plan and direct studies to investigate human or animal disease, preventive methods, and treatments for disease.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide expertise in the design, management and evaluation of study protocols and health status questionnaires, sample selection, and analysis.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write articles for publication in professional journals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases and their impact on public policies, scientific studies, or surveys.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Write grant applications to fund epidemiologic research.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan, administer and evaluate health safety standards and programs to improve public health, conferring with health department, industry personnel, physicians, and others.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, government health officials and others regarding medical applications of sciences, such as physics, biology, and chemistry.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise professional, technical, and clerical personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach principles of medicine and medical and laboratory procedures to physicians, residents, students, and technicians.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and analyze samples to study effects of drugs, gases, pesticides, or microorganisms on cell structure and tissue.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
